texto: The human heart will never wrinkle.
explicacao: Citação de Marie de Rabutin-Chantal de Sévigné sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “The human heart will never wrinkle.” articula reflexao, wisdom-quote, human em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: It appears to me that in spite of myself I have been dragged to this inevitable point where old age must be undergone. I see it there before me; I have reached it; and I should at least like so to arrange matters that I do not move on, that I do not travel farther along this path of infirmities, pains, losses of memory and disfigurement. Their attack is at hand, and I hear a voice that says, `You must go along, whatever you may say; or if indeed you will not, then you must die,' which is an extremity from which nature recoils. However, that is the fate of all who go on a little too far.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marie de Rabutin-Chantal de Sévigné: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “It appears to me that in spite of myself I have been dragged to this inevitable point where old a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, aging, along pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e luto.

texto: If you are not feeling well, if you have not slept, chocolate will revive you. But you have no chocolate! I think of that again and again! My dear, how will you ever manage?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Marie de Rabutin-Chantal de Sévigné que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “If you are not feeling well, if you have not slept, chocolate will revive you. But you have no ch…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, dessert, humour, com foco em humour.
